Interface Hitbox2D

All Known Implementing Classes:
AlwaysHitbox2D, AndHitbox2D, NeverHitbox2D, NotHitbox2D, OrHitbox2D, ShapeHitbox2D, TransformedHitbox2D
public sealed interface Hitbox2D permits AlwaysHitbox2D, AndHitbox2D, NeverHitbox2D, NotHitbox2D, OrHitbox2D, ShapeHitbox2D, TransformedHitbox2D
Represents a 2D hitbox used for collision and interaction detection.

A hitbox defines a region in 2D space that can:

  • Check if a point is inside (contains(Point2D))
  • Detect overlap with another hitbox (intersects(Hitbox2D))

Design Notes

  • Hitboxes may be infinite (e.g. always()) — always check isFinite() when needed
  • Bounds may not exist (e.g. infinite or complex shapes) — getBounds() returns Optional
  • Implementations should be immutable and thread-safe where possible
See Also:
  • shape(Shape)
  • or(Hitbox2D...)
  • and(Hitbox2D...)
  • not()
  • never()
  • always()

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static Hitbox2D
    always()
    Returns a 2D hitbox that covers all points.
    default Hitbox2D
    and(boolean optimize, Hitbox2D hb)
    Intersects this hitbox with the given one and returns the intersection.
    static Hitbox2D
    and(boolean optimize, Hitbox2D... hitboxes)
    Creates a 2D hitbox that is an intersection of the given hitboxes.
    default Hitbox2D
    and(Hitbox2D hb)
    Intersects this hitbox with the given one and returns the optimized intersection.
    static Hitbox2D
    and(Hitbox2D... hitboxes)
    Creates a 2D hitbox that is an intersection of the given hitboxes.
    boolean
    contains(Hitbox2D other)
    Checks whether this hitbox fully contains the given one.
    boolean
    contains(Point2D point)
    Checks whether the given point lies within this hitbox.
    Optional<Area>
    createArea()
    Creates an Area representing all points covered by this hitbox, if it's finite, otherwise returns an empty optional.
    default Optional<Area>
    createArea(AffineTransform affine)
    Creates an Area representing all points covered by this hitbox and applies the given AffineTransform instance to it.
    default boolean
    equals(Hitbox2D other)
    Returns whether this hitbox and the given one cover exactly the same points.
    Optional<Rectangle2D>
    getBounds()
    Returns a rectangle that completely encloses this hitbox, if any.
    default Optional<Rectangle2D>
    getBounds(AffineTransform affine)
    Returns a rectangle that completely encloses this hitbox transformed using the provided AffineTransform instance.
    boolean
    intersects(Hitbox2D other)
    Checks whether this hitbox intersects with the given one.
    boolean
    isFinite()
    Determines whether this hitbox represents a finite region.
    static Hitbox2D
    never()
    Returns a 2D hitbox that doesn't cover any points.
    default Hitbox2D
    not()
    Creates a 2D hitbox that covers every point except those covered by this hitbox.
    default Hitbox2D
    or(boolean optimize, Hitbox2D hb)
    Unites this hitbox with the given one and returns the union.
    static Hitbox2D
    or(boolean optimize, Hitbox2D... hitboxes)
    Creates a 2D hitbox that is a union of the given hitboxes.
    default Hitbox2D
    or(Hitbox2D hb)
    Unites this hitbox with the given one and returns the optimized union.
    static Hitbox2D
    or(Hitbox2D... hitboxes)
    Creates a 2D hitbox that is a union of the given hitboxes.
    static Hitbox2D
    shape(Shape shape)
    Creates a 2D hitbox matching the given shape without rotating the shape.
    default Hitbox2D
    transform(AffineTransform affine)
    Transforms this hitbox using the given AffineTransform instance.

  • Method Details

    • contains

      boolean contains(Point2D point)
      Checks whether the given point lies within this hitbox.
      Parameters:
      point - the point to test
      Returns:
      whether the point is contained by this hitbox

    • intersects

      boolean intersects(Hitbox2D other)
      Checks whether this hitbox intersects with the given one.

      Intersection is symmetric: a.intersects(b) == b.intersects(a)

      Parameters:
      other - the other hitbox, must not be null
      Returns:
      whether the hitboxes overlap

    • contains

      boolean contains(Hitbox2D other)
      Checks whether this hitbox fully contains the given one.

      Containment is not always symmetric, unlike intersection. It's symmetric only for equal hitboxes; otherwise, it's asymmetric.

      A finite hitbox can never contain an infinite one. If this hitbox and the given one are infinite, this method checks for equality instead.

      Parameters:
      other - the other hitbox, must not be null
      Returns:
      whether this hitbox fully contains the given one

    • equals

      default boolean equals(Hitbox2D other)
      Returns whether this hitbox and the given one cover exactly the same points.

      This method may return false negative results. False positives must not occur.

      Parameters:
      other - the other hitbox
      Returns:
      whether the two hitboxes are equivalent

    • isFinite

      boolean isFinite()
      Determines whether this hitbox represents a finite region.

      Example: always() returns false, while a rectangle returns true.

      Returns:
      whether the hitbox is finite

    • getBounds

      Optional<Rectangle2D> getBounds()
      Returns a rectangle that completely encloses this hitbox, if any.

      Typically returns empty for infinite hitboxes.

      Returns:
      the bounding box, or empty if undefined
      See Also:
      • getBounds(AffineTransform)

    • getBounds

      default Optional<Rectangle2D> getBounds(AffineTransform affine)
      Returns a rectangle that completely encloses this hitbox transformed using the provided AffineTransform instance.
      Parameters:
      affine - the transform
      Returns:
      the bounding box after transformation, or empty if undefined
      See Also:
      • getBounds()

    • createArea

      Optional<Area> createArea()
      Creates an Area representing all points covered by this hitbox, if it's finite, otherwise returns an empty optional.
      Returns:
      a new area of this hitbox, if applicable
      See Also:
      • createArea(AffineTransform)

    • createArea

      default Optional<Area> createArea(AffineTransform affine)
      Creates an Area representing all points covered by this hitbox and applies the given AffineTransform instance to it.
      Parameters:
      affine - the transform
      Returns:
      a new area of this hitbox, if applicable
      See Also:
      • createArea()

    • shape

      static Hitbox2D shape(Shape shape)
      Creates a 2D hitbox matching the given shape without rotating the shape.
      Parameters:
      shape - the shape
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox

    • or

      static Hitbox2D or(Hitbox2D... hitboxes)
      Creates a 2D hitbox that is a union of the given hitboxes. The hitbox list is optimized (all hitboxes that are fully contained by other hitboxes in the list are removed).
      Parameters:
      hitboxes - the hitboxes
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox
      See Also:
      • or(boolean, Hitbox2D...)

    • or

      static Hitbox2D or(boolean optimize, Hitbox2D... hitboxes)
      Creates a 2D hitbox that is a union of the given hitboxes.
      Parameters:
      optimize - whether to optimize the hitbox list (removes all hitboxes that are fully contained by other hitboxes in the list)
      hitboxes - the hitboxes
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox

    • and

      static Hitbox2D and(Hitbox2D... hitboxes)
      Creates a 2D hitbox that is an intersection of the given hitboxes. The hitbox list is optimized (all hitboxes that fully contain any other hitbox in the list are removed).
      Parameters:
      hitboxes - the hitboxes
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox
      See Also:
      • and(boolean, Hitbox2D...)

    • and

      static Hitbox2D and(boolean optimize, Hitbox2D... hitboxes)
      Creates a 2D hitbox that is an intersection of the given hitboxes.
      Parameters:
      optimize - whether to optimize the hitbox list (removes all hitboxes that fully contain any other hitbox in the list)
      hitboxes - the hitboxes
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox

    • and

      default Hitbox2D and(Hitbox2D hb)
      Intersects this hitbox with the given one and returns the optimized intersection.
      Parameters:
      hb - the hitbox to intersect with
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox
      See Also:
      • and(Hitbox2D...)

    • and

      default Hitbox2D and(boolean optimize, Hitbox2D hb)
      Intersects this hitbox with the given one and returns the intersection.
      Parameters:
      optimize - whether to optimize hitboxes
      hb - the hitbox to intersect with
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox
      See Also:
      • and(boolean, Hitbox2D...)

    • or

      default Hitbox2D or(Hitbox2D hb)
      Unites this hitbox with the given one and returns the optimized union.
      Parameters:
      hb - the hitbox to unite with
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox
      See Also:
      • or(Hitbox2D...)

    • or

      default Hitbox2D or(boolean optimize, Hitbox2D hb)
      Unites this hitbox with the given one and returns the union.
      Parameters:
      optimize - whether to optimize hitboxes
      hb - the hitbox to unite with
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox
      See Also:
      • or(boolean, Hitbox2D...)

    • not

      default Hitbox2D not()
      Creates a 2D hitbox that covers every point except those covered by this hitbox. In other words, returns a negation/complement of this hitbox.
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox

    • transform

      default Hitbox2D transform(AffineTransform affine)
      Transforms this hitbox using the given AffineTransform instance.
      Parameters:
      affine - the transform object
      Returns:
      a new 2D hitbox

    • never

      static Hitbox2D never()
      Returns a 2D hitbox that doesn't cover any points. Useful for purely visual objects, like particles, etc.

      It doesn't have a bounding box and returns an empty Area.

      Returns:
      an empty 2D hitbox

    • always

      static Hitbox2D always()
      Returns a 2D hitbox that covers all points.

      It doesn't have a bounding box or an Area.

      Returns:
      an "all-points" 2D hitbox
